reason: The text contains a prophetic vision by Micaiah describing an upcoming military disaster orchestrated by Yahweh. Specifically, Micaiah prophesies: 'I saw all Israel scattered upon the mountains, as sheep that have no shepherd' (וְרָאִ֤יתִי אֶת־ כָּל־ יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ נְפוֹצִ֣ים עַל־ הֶֽהָרִ֔ים כַּצֹּ֕אן אֲשֶׁ֥ר אֵין־ לָהֶ֖ן רֹעֶ֑ה). This vision describes a supernatural destruction by Yahweh that is not individual but affects the general public (all of Israel's forces). The prophecy is subsequently fulfilled when the battle against Arameans results in Israel's defeat and the death of King Ahab. The text clearly indicates this is an upcoming, divinely-orchestrated disaster affecting the general population.
